items.q: What is the denary value of binary 10110?
items.why: Students forget to apply place values. Each position is a power of 2 from right (1, 2, 4, 8, 16...). Reading binary as denary is the most common error.

items.q: What is the key difference between a compiler and an interpreter?
items.correct: A compiler translates the whole program first; an interpreter executes line by line.
items.misconception: A compiler is a person, an interpreter is software.
items.why: Students confuse the human meanings with technical ones. In CS, both are programs. Compilers produce an executable; interpreters run source directly each time.

items.correct: The ability of different classes to respond to the same method call in their own way.
items.misconception: Having multiple classes with the same name.
items.random_wrong: Variables that change type at runtime.
items.why: Students confuse polymorphism with naming. Polymorphism means 'many forms' - a single interface (method signature) behaving differently depending on the object's actual class.
Correct answer: Microsoft Word. “Linux kernel” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
It's Has visible source code. “Always free of charge” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many main categories of software are taught at GCSE (system and application)?
items.why: The real figure is 2 categories. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
